prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene was performed using DFT with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral state), with cation and anion at multiplicity 2. The calculation employed PySCF as the backend and computed Fukui function indices (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity, and f⁰ as the dual descriptor) through finite-difference analysis of Mulliken partial charges across three charge states. The ipso-carbon (C directly bonded to NO₂) is the most electrophilic site with f⁺ = 0.496, while the ortho-carbon is the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.107. All three states converged successfully, and charge conservation checks confirmed the validity of the indices (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ each = 1.0000). The calculation reflects the expected deactivating effect of the electron-withdrawing nitro group on the benzene ring.
